This watch features the ever popular Tiffany blue turquoise made of sterling silver, in a lovely and intricate stainless steel case. description: Up for grabs is one of the most underrated independent watch brands of recent years, Parmigiani Fleurier. This particular model, the Tonda PF Micro-Rotor was made in collaboration with none other than Wei Koh from Revolution and The Rake. This watch features a hand-guilloche dial in a lovely salmon color. title: Parmigiani Fleurier Tonda PF GMT Rattrapante
description: Offered here is a stunning example of the Parmigiani Fleurier Tonda PF GMT Rattrapante in stainless steel & platinum, featuring their gorgeous blue guilloché dial. With this new movement, Parmigiani applied rattrapante split-seconds technology to a dual-time hand for the very first time, and the result is one of the innovative travel-time complication in modern watch making. Two superimposed skeletonised hands sit on the dial — one white gold for local time, one rose gold for home time. A press

title: Kudoke 1
description: Offered here is a very rare and beautiful watch from the German independent outfit, Kudoke. The Kudoke 1 is Stefan Kudokes first flagship model, this fitted with a simple yet stunning silver dial. These watches are extremely desirable, and sell out upon release; there is an alleged 12-month wait list just for the opportunity to be considered to purchase one. This Kudoke 1 remains unworn/new, and comes complete with its original Kudoke boxes and paperwork, as sold new.

title: Vacheron Constantin “LNIB 4020T” Traditionnelle
description: Up for grabs is a breathtaking like-new, relatively unworn Vacheron Constantin Traditionnelle, reference 4020T, sold in 2022. This happens to be one of our favorite models from Vacheron, and features a skeletonized triple-date calendar with moonphase, housed in 18kt rose gold. This watch comes with its original VC Warranty card, booklet, and box.

description: Up for grabs is a rare and fun Rolex Day-Date housed in 18kt Rose gold, reference 116595RBOW, fitted with a lovely full-diamond pave dial, contrasted with 10 baguette-cut rainbow-coloured sapphire hour markers, and the ever popular rainbow sapphire bezel. This watch also features the rare version with pave diamonds running down the middle of the bracelet. description: Up for grabs is one of MB&Fs most unique creations, the Horological Machine N°7 AquaPod, in its rarest iteration — Platinum Red, limited to just 25 pieces worldwide. Inspired by the jellyfish, the AquaPod is built around a radially symmetric, concentric architecture with a 60-second flying tourbillon crowning the movement under a massive domed sapphire crystal. title: MB&F Legacy Machine Perpetual
description: Offered here is MB&Fs flagship model of the Legacy Machine line the Legacy Machine perpetual calendar, that was done in collaboration with Irish independent watchmaker Stephen McDonnell. Visually, the LM Perpetual is unmistakable with its large, suspended balance wheel floating above the dial, with a striking perpetual calendar mechanism displayed in a three-dimensional layout. title: MB&F Horological Machine HM8 “Can-AM”
description: Offered here is one of MB&Fs avant-garde timepieces with a striking, unconventional design that exemplifies MB&Fs commitment to pushing the boundaries of traditional watchmaking. The HM8 was given the moniker “Can-Am”, an automotive inspired timepiece with a design inspired by automotive parts such as a cars suspension, and the time transmitted on its side as a drivers watch with its speedometer-type display. title: Rolex “6262 Paul Newman” Daytona
description: Offered here is an incredible example of Rolex Daytona, Ref. 6262, circa 1970, fitted with an exotic dial, nicknamed the Paul Newman Daytona, due to the fact that the famous actor favored wearing the Rolex Daytona with the exotic dial, and can be seen in many photographs wearing the watch. description: Offered here is a beautiful and rare Rolex GMT-Master, reference 1675/8 in 18kt yellow gold, circa 1970. The 1675/8 is the second iteration of the all-gold GMT-Master, following its introduction in the late 1950s in the reference 6542. Further, this particular example comes complete with its original Rolex box, and two sets of punched Warranty Certificates, originally sold in 1972. The condition on this Rolex GMT-Master is great. title: Rolex “6239 Paul Newman” Daytona
description: Offered here is a lovely example of Rolex Daytona, Ref. 6239, circa 1968, fit with an exotic dial, nicknamed the Paul Newman Daytona, due to the fact that the famous actor favored wearing the Rolex Daytona with the exotic dial, and can be seen in many photographs wearing the watch. This example is outfitted with a tri-color reverse-panda dial and pump pushers. The condition of this Daytona is great. title: Movado “M90 19028” Chronograph
description: This is a super rare and crispy Movado Cal. M90 Chronograph, circa 1940s, reference 19048. This watch is highly desirable due the radium tachymeter dial and popular screwback FB case. This case shares many characteristics that were used on Patek Philippe chronographs, such as the pushers, and is highly coveted amongst collectors. The watch itself is in excellent condition. title: Audemars Piguet “5043BA” Square Watch
description: Up for grabs is a cool and rare Audemars Piguet square watch in yellow gold, given the reference 5034BA. This particular watch, according to an Extract of the Archives of Audemars Piguet, was produced in May of 1963. The condition of this quirky vintage Audemars Piguet is great, retaining a strong case with a crisp hallmark on the underside of the lugs, and a well-preserved dial, showing light wear over the years.

title: Rolex “Zenith Floating 16520” Daytona
description: Up for grabs is a lovely example of the incredibly popular Rolex Zenith Daytona, so-called for its movement made by the brand, Zenith. Rolex introduced the automatic movement chronograph in 1987, using a movement manufactured by Zenith, as it was the industry’s only self-winding chronograph movement that met Rolex’s high quality standards. title: Patek Philippe “2594J” Disco Volante
description: Up for grabs is a very cool Patek Philippe Disco Volante, reference 2594 in yellow gold. These Disco Volante Pateks were produced in the late 1950s and in limited amounts. The condition of this Patek Philippe Disco Volante is excellent. The case appears unpolished and sharp (albeit worn from use over the years), with a visible hallmark on the side of the case. Further, the dial is in lovely condition, unrestored with almost no wear to it.

title: Omega “145.022-69BA” Speedmaster
description: Up for grabs is a 1969 Omega Speedmaster in 18kt yellow gold (reference 145.022-69BA). These yellow gold speedmasters were a limited production piece with only 1,014 watches ever produced. The first 32 watches were gifted to astronauts, politicians, and other personalities. The other examples were sold to the public and numbered the present example was numbered 902, and originally delivered to Mexico in 1970 according to an Extract from the Archives of Omega (included in the sale). title: Eberhard “Gilt” Scafograf 300 Diver
description: Up for grabs is a rare and highly sought-after Eberhard Scafograf 300, with uncommon gilt inlay text, produced in the mid-1960s. This example is in amazing vintage condition. The twisted-lugs case does not show signs of polishing, and still has defined bevels in the lugs. The dial is still very glossy with minimal wear from aging. The bezel shows minimal wear, overall very well preserved. Further, the dial still retains its original lume, aged to a nice golden yellow, and matching in the hands.

title: Rolex “Full-Set 1016” Explorer
description: Up for grabs is a Rolex Explorer I, Reference 1016, circa 1987 (R-serial) toward the very end of production, which comes complete as a full-set, with its original Rolex box, punched Warranty Certificate, and Explorer booklet, and a recent service by Rolex (including RSC card, still under warranty). This particular Explorer is in great condition. The case has been lightly polished, but still retains thick and even lugs. title: Patek Philippe “3587/1” Beta 21
description: Offered here is a rare Patek Phillipe, reference 3587/1, circa 1970, during what is now known of as the quartz crisis. Only 6000 Beta 21 movements were made, split amongst other brands, such as Rolex and Piaget; Patek utilized allegedly less than 500 of these movements. This design deviated from Pateks usual dressed-down elegance the watch is a staggering 43mm, and wears like a modern, contemporary watch on the wrist. description: Up for grabs is the Breguet Type XX Transatlantique Ref. 3820ST/H2/SW9, a modern interpretation of the brand’s historic aviation chronographs. The Type XX was originally commissioned by the French government in 1954, Breguet supplied these watches to the Air Force, Naval Air Arm, and CEV. At the heart of the watch is its signature flyback chronograph, a complication engineered for pilots just like the originals. This particular Omega Speedmaster remains in nice used condition, showing light wear from use over the years. Further, it remains complete, with its original Omega Speedmaster box, Warranty Cards, and booklet.

This watch has been nicknamed the Kasparov due to the famous chess Grandmaster Garry Kasparov, who would wear this model during his professional games, and would always remove it from his wrist prior to his last, winning moves. description: Offered here is a stunning and extremely low production DeBethune DB25 know better as the Starry Varius. title: Rolex “Full-Set 228349RBR Pave” Day-Date 40

The watch itself is in great pre-owned condition, showing light wear from use to the case (the case can be detailed upon request).

This watch comes super complete, with its original Roger Dubuis box, two Warranty Certificates (dating to 1999), Besancon Observatory Timing Certificate, extra solid caseback, and original hangtag, and matching RD white gold tang buckle. This reference is a factory bejeweled extension of the mid-size 14790 line, featuring factory diamonds on the bezel, bracelet, and baguette-cut stones on the center links. description: Offered for sale is a very rare Audemars Piguet Tourbillon watch from their Jules Audemars Reserve de Marche line, reference 25718PT, circa 1990s, housed in a platinum case with openworked or skeletonized dial. The watch itself is in great vintage condition, unpolished with minor signs of wear. The watch comes with its original Audemars Piguet matching tang buckle.

description: Offered here is an exceedingly rare Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, reference 25831PT, produced circa 1997, to commemorate the 25th anniversary of the Royal Oak. This model was housed in a 40mm platinum case, and fit with a Caliber 2875 movement, featuring tourbillon, power reserve, and day of the month complications. title: Audemars Piguet “25718OR Tourbillon” Jules Audemars
description: Offered for sale is a very rare Audemars Piguet Tourbillon watch from their Jules Audemars Reserve de Marche line, reference 25718OR, circa 1990s, housed in rose gold case with matching rose gold dial. This model is fitted in a 39mm case, and features a date, power reserve (reserve de marche) and tourbillon complications. The watch itself is in great vintage condition, unpolished with minor signs of wear. The watch comes with its original Audemars Piguet matching tang buckle.

description: Offered here is a very rare and beautiful watch from the German independent outfit, Kudoke. The Kudoke 2 is a continuation of the first Kudoke 24-Hour model, beautifully hand-crafted with a stunning silver/white dial and day-date 24-hour indicator disk at 12 oclock. title: IWC “Full-Set IW322701” Ingenieur
description: Up for grabs is an IWC Ingenieur black dial, in stainless steel. The Ingenieur line, a collection originally from the 1950s as an anti-magnetic watch for engineers and scientists is IWCs marquee integrated bracelet sports watch. This particular Ingenieur is in like great used condition, carefully worn a handful of times by the original owner. The watch comes complete with its original IWC box, Warranty Card, and all accessories as new including an extra leather strap.

title: Tag Heuer “BNIB Fragment Design” Formula 1
description: Offered here is a special edition Tag Heuer Carrera, made in collaboration with Fragment Design, released in 2020. Limited to 500 pieces, this watch is a modern take on vintage Heuer designs, particularly the Viceroy Autavia, featuring a steel bracelet and Fragments iconic lightning bolt logo. This example is brand new, never worn or sized, and complete with its original Tag Heuer box, warranty card, and all accessories.

title: Patek Philippe “Full-Set 5450P” Annual Calendar Advanced Research
description: Up for grabs is one you dont see everyday the Philippe Annual Calendar, reference 5450P with a salmon dial. The reference 5450P features the highly technical caliber 324 S IRM QA LU with the pulsomax escapement, made from silinvar (silicon), which dramatically reduces friction, improves energy efficiency, and enhances long-term precision without lubrication. title: H. Moser Cie. “BNIB 6902-1200” Streamliner Chronograph
description: Up for grabs is a limited edition H. Moser & Cie Streamliner flyback Chronograph, mid in only 100 pieces. Originally launched in 2019 as the brands first chronograph wristwatch, the streamliner has become Mosers quintessential sports watch. This example is brand new, never worn or sized, and complete with its original Moser box, warranty card, and all accessories.

However, due to import restrictions (the US Government would only award contracts to those products produced in the USA), Blancpain was unable to sell the Fifty Fathoms to the US Navy. title: Audemars Piguet “25902PT Skeleton Tourbillon” Royal Oak
description: Offered here is an exceedingly rare Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, reference 25902PT, produced circa early 2000s. According to Audemars Piguet, only approximately 11 pieces of this reference were made over a 10 year period, of which 6 were in platinum, making this one of the lowest production Royal Oaks made. title: Rolex “1803 Rose Gold Arabic” Day-Date
description: Up for grabs is a breathtaking and incredibly rare Rolex Day-Date, reference 1803, circa 1960. This particular Day-Date was produced in 18kt rose gold, and made specifically for the Arab/Arabic market, fit with an Arabic dial and day/date disks. Very few of these special Day-Dates are known to exist, and very rarely come to market. This particular example may be the best example of this configuration known and was discovered fresh-to-market last year in a small jewelry shop in upstate New York.

title: Rolex “6204” Submariner
description: Up for grabs we have something very special the very first reference Rolex Submariner, reference 6204, which Rolex introduced in 1953. The reference 6204 was produced only for a short, one-year period, before they transitioned to the next iteration reference 6205. The earliest 6204 Submariners are discernible by its lack of depth rating, with simply the word Submariner printed at the bottom. title: Blancpain “Demo MilSpec 1” Fifty Fathoms
description: In the early 1950s, an elite unit of the French Navy, responsible for their own diving equipment and not satisfied with any of the current diving watches on the market, set out to make their own watch that they deemed suitable for diving, known as the Frogmen. The result was an inevitable partnership with Blancpain for the creation of the watch we know as the Fifty Fathoms. title: Rolex Daytona “Rusty” By Artisans De Geneve
description: It’s no secret that we am not the biggest fans of modified watches, but occasionally there are some that spark our interest. Some may say that it is sacrilegious to completely rebuild a new Daytona, but Artisans De Geneve is a brand that modifies watches better than anyone. We like to think of them as the Singer of the watch world. title: Spaceone Watches “BNIB Tellurium”
description: Offered here is the Spaceone Tellurium, and its not your average Kickstarter watch. Guillaume Laidet, the man behind the revival of Nivada Grenchen and Vulcain, set out to make his own brand in collaboration with independent watchmaker Théo Auffret. Spaceone started as Argon on Kickstarter, and quickly grew in popularity with their futuristic (space age) designs and innovative complications. title: Rolex “Full-Set 18388 Pave & Ruby” Day-Date
description: Up for grabs is a stunning and very rare Rolex Day-Date, reference 18388, circa 1994, with a factory pave diamond and ruby dial, together with diamond-set in the bezel and lugs. These factory diamond and bejeweled watches were considered Rolexs top of the line and ornate offerings, and remain to this day, usually reserved for top clients and not easily ordered by the general public. This particular example comes complete with its original Rolex Presidential box and punched Warranty Certificate.

title: IWC “BNIB IW501001” Big Pilot
description: Up for grabs is a new unworn, 2022 IWCBig Pilot, in stainless steel. This particular IWC features an 8day power reserve in the classic pilots case. Thios watch is in new condition, bought new in the summer of 2022, and never worn. The watch comes complete with its original IWC box, Warranty Card, and all accessories as new.

The Concept line is Audemars Piguets attempt at a mixture of haute horology of the highest order, mixed with a futuristic, avant-garde style. The watch itself is in used condition, with some wear on the case, bezel and buckle, his particular example comes watch only.

title: Rolex “Salmon 2508” Chronograph

This particular 2508 is a second generation example, denoted by its 35mm case with square button pushers. Further, this example features a lovely salmon-color sector-snail dial, with both telemeter and tachymetre scales. This Rolex chronograph is in good vintage condition. description: Up for grabs is a 2019 Patek Philippe Aquanaut, reference 5168G, housed in 18kt white gold, and fitted with a green khaki dial and matching strap. This Patek Philippe Aquanaut remains in excellent pre-owned condition, with light signs of wear from use; the strap has been sized to fit an approximate 7 wrist.
